description: Canonical policy for the bounded Fable 5 pilot (UTV2-1569, following the pilot-evaluation issue's PILOT LONGER / lean YES / narrow scope conclusion). This file is the sole source of truth for which trigger classes, caps, and skip conditions govern Fable 5 eligibility as an advisory-only planning/review model. scripts/ops/planning-model-routing.ts is the sole mechanical consumer -- do not duplicate these values elsewhere. Mirrors the Codex model-routing policy convention (docs/05_operations/policies/codex-model-routing.json).
pilot_enabled_description: Global mechanical kill switch, independent of docs/05_operations/FABLE_PILOT_STATE.json's own status field. When false, scripts/ops/planning-model-routing.ts#resolvePlanningModel and #resolveFableAdvisoryReview must NEVER return a Fable model, regardless of pilot state, trigger class, or caller input. Both this flag AND the pilot state's status=='active' are required for Fable eligibility -- either one being false/off fails closed to Sonnet. This is the primary lever scripts/ops/fable-pilot-rollback.ts flips to false.

repeated_architecture_bounce.description: A lane has bounced CHANGES_REQUIRED more than once on the same architectural question (2+ same-question bounces).

live_state_root_cause.description: A hard root-cause/mechanistic-verification task where live-state evidence (runtime, DB, CI) must be checked, not just reasoned about.

product_synthesis_no_precedent.description: A product-synthesis decision with no existing precedent to follow -- judgment-heavy, not mechanically derivable from prior work.

build_mode_certification_review.description: Advisory review of a Build Mode planning artifact or certification packet before it reaches Griff. Never itself a certifying vote.

skip_list_description: Fable is never eligible for these classes even if a caller supplies a trigger_class string -- resolvePlanningModel treats any of these as an automatic Sonnet result, not a fallback. These are explicitly out of scope per UTV2-1569's required outcome.
